meta.question: What does "APS" stand for in this model and what is its mission role?
meta.finalAnswer:
In this TMT model, **APS stands for "Alignment and Phasing System"**.

Its mission role is described as follows:

The Alignment and Phasing System (APS) is a Shack-Hartmann wavefront sensor responsible for the overall pre-adaptive-optics wavefront quality of the Thirty Meter Telescope (TMT). In order to produce wavefronts of acceptable quality, APS will adjust the following parameters as required: segment pistons and tip/tilts, segment surface figure (via warping harness adjustments), 2 degrees of M3 rigid body motion, piston of M2, and either tip/tilt or x/y translation of M2.

The purpose of the APS is not to verify that individual optics, such as M2 and M3, have met their respective surface requirements, but rather to align the telescope.

The APS design and concept is based upon that of the Phasing Camera System (PCS), which fulfilled a similar role for the Keck Observatory telescopes.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?elem ?type ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name . 
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("APS"))) 
  ?elem rdf:type ?type .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: Found multiple APS-related elements. The path shows "Alignment and Phasing System" - need to explore the APS Mission block to find its mission role description.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?prop (COUNT(*) as ?count) WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name . 
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("APS Mission"))) 
  ?elem ?prop ?value .
} 
GROUP BY ?prop 
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 25

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?comment ?body WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name "APS%20Mission" .
  ?elem rdf:type vocab:block .
  ?comment vocab:Comment_annotatedElement ?elem .
  ?comment vocab:Comment_body ?body .
}
LIMIT 10

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?ownedElem ?name ?type WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name "APS%20Mission" .
  ?elem rdf:type vocab:block .
  ?elem vocab:Element_ownedElement ?ownedElem .
  ?ownedElem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
  ?ownedElem rdf:type ?type .
}
LIMIT 30

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?diagram ?name ?context WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name "APS%20Mission" .
  ?elem rdf:type vocab:block .
  ?diagram vocab:Diagram_context ?elem .
  ?diagram vocab:NamedElement_name ?name .
}
LIMIT 10

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?view ?name ?owner WHERE {
  ?view vocab:NamedElement_name "APS Mission" .
  ?view rdf:type vocab:view .
  ?view vocab:Diagram_ownerOfDiagram ?owner .
}
LIMIT 10

LIMIT 50

SELECT ?body WHERE {

  ?view rdf:type vocab:view .
  ?comment vocab:Comment_annotatedElement ?view .
  ?comment vocab:Comment_body ?body .

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>

SELECT ?body WHERE {

  ?view rdf:type vocab:view .
  ?view vocab:Element_ownedComment ?comment .
  ?comment vocab:Comment_body ?body .

BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?body WHERE {

  ?view rdf:type vocab:view .
  ?view vocab:Element_ownedComment ?comment .
  ?comment vocab:Comment_body ?body .
}
LIMIT 10

request.reason: Found the description of APS in the model. The view "The APS and Its Role in TMT" contains a detailed comment explaining what APS stands for and its mission role.

request.answer:
In this TMT model, **APS stands for "Alignment and Phasing System"**.

Its mission role is described as follows:

The Alignment and Phasing System (APS) is a Shack-Hartmann wavefront sensor responsible for the overall pre-adaptive-optics wavefront quality of the Thirty Meter Telescope (TMT). In order to produce wavefronts of acceptable quality, APS will adjust the following parameters as required: segment pistons and tip/tilts, segment surface figure (via warping harness adjustments), 2 degrees of M3 rigid body motion, piston of M2, and either tip/tilt or x/y translation of M2.

The purpose of the APS is not to verify that individual optics, such as M2 and M3, have met their respective surface requirements, but rather to align the telescope.

The APS design and concept is based upon that of the Phasing Camera System (PCS), which fulfilled a similar role for the Keck Observatory telescopes.
